meta.title: Poetic Hand-Gesture Images · 手勢圖 shǒushì tú
meta.intro: The classical handbooks pair each finger technique with a poetic nature-image and a four-character verse introduced by "興曰 Xìng yuē" — the Book of Songs metaphor device. The Taiyin Daquanji set has 33 illustrations (17 right-hand, then 16 left-hand). Below, each gesture is shown with the composed jianzipu glyph from the bundled Qin font, the Chinese verse where it is in the public domain, a paraphrased theme, and a link to view the original woodblock and full translation on silkqin.com.
meta.imageRightsNote: The woodblock prints are historical (likely public-domain artworks), but the photographic reproductions and English translations on silkqin.com are John Thompson's work and are not copied here — follow the source link to view them.

gestures.image_title: Lone duck looks back at the flock
gestures.theme: Longing; the solitary bird turning back toward its companions — the middle finger drawing inward.

gestures.image_title: The howling ape climbing the tall tree
gestures.verse_hanzi: 瞻彼號猿，升於喬木。欲上不上，其勢逐逐
gestures.verse_pinyin: zhān bǐ háo yuán, shēng yú qiáo mù; yù shàng bù shàng, qí shì zhú zhú
gestures.theme: Behold the howling ape climbing the tall tree; wishing to go up yet not up, its momentum pressing on — the wide, surging vibrato.
Likened to a cicada singing in autumn — a fine, shimmering vibrato that swells then fades.
Index finger flicking outward — one of the seventeen right-hand gestures.
Index finger drawing inward — one of the seventeen right-hand gestures.
Ring finger striking inward — one of the seventeen right-hand gestures.
Two strings pinched together into one sound — one of the seventeen right-hand gestures.
A quick up-and-back of the pressing finger, made with the pluck — one of the sixteen left-hand gestures.
